summary:
The provided retrieval results indicate there are no documented studies evaluating whether HER2 immunoreactivity influences outcomes in locally advanced urothelial carcinoma patients receiving adjuvant M-VEC chemotherapy. As such, based solely on available evidence, HER2 immunoreactivity has neither been shown nor proposed as a clinically useful prognostic marker within this therapeutic context.

The summary should be concise and strictly limited to two sentences only:

Final Summary: 
HER2 immunoreactivity has not been demonstrated to have any significant prognostic value in locally advanced urothelial carcinoma patients receiving adjuvant M-VEC chemotherapy according to current evidence. Traditional pathological features and PD-L1 expression remain the primary determinants of patient outcomes in this population.

HER2 immunoreactivity has not been demonstrated to have any significant prognostic value in locally advanced urothelial carcinoma patients receiving adjuvant M-VEC chemotherapy according to current evidence. Traditional pathological features and PD-L1 expression remain the primary determinants of patient outcomes in this population.
